aeon: noun. Commonwealth standard spelling of eon.
aero: adjective. Of or pertaining to aviation.
age: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Angal.
ane: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xârâcùù.
anger: noun. A strong and unpleasant feeling of displeasure, hostility, or antagonism, usually combined with an urge to yell, curse, damage or
are: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Western Arrernte.
argon: noun. The chemical element (symbol Ar) with an atomic number of 18.
ave: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Avestan.
aver: verb. To assert the truth of (something); to affirm (something) with confidence; to declare (something) in a positive manner.
avo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Agavotaguerra.
ear: noun. The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna or auricle, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
earn: verb. To gain (success, reward, recognition) through applied effort or work.
ego: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eggon.
eng: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for English.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.

erg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sie.

gan: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gan.

gear: noun. Equipment or paraphernalia, especially that used for an athletic endeavor.
gen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Geman Deng.
genoa: noun. A staysail that resembles a jib but extends aft beyond the mast.
genro: noun. A body of elder statesmen of Japan, formerly used as informal advisors to the Emperor.
gerah: noun. An ancient Hebrew unit of weight and currency, one twentieth of a shekel.
gherao: noun. A protest in which a group of people surrounds a politician, building, etc. until demands are met.

gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
gore: noun. Blood, especially that from a wound when thickened due to exposure to the air.

hag: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hanga.
hang: verb. To be or remain suspended.
hanger: noun. One who hangs, or causes to be hanged; a hangman, paper hanger, etc.
hangover: noun. Negative effects, such as headache or nausea, caused by previous drunkenness due to (excessive) consumption of alcohol.
hao: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hakö.
hare: noun. Any of several plant-eating mammals of the genus Lepus, similar to a rabbit, but larger and with longer ears.
haven: noun. A harbour or anchorage protected from the sea.
hear: verb. To perceive sounds through the ear.
hen: noun. A female chicken (Gallus gallus), especially a sexually mature one kept for her eggs.
her: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Herero.

hoe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Horom.

hon: noun. A term of endearment; Honey, sweetheart
hone: noun. A sharpening stone composed of extra-fine grit used for removing the burr or curl from the blade of a razor or some other edge tool.
hora: noun. A circle dance popular in the Balkans, Israel and Yiddish culture worldwide.
horn: noun. A hard growth of keratin that protrudes from the top of the head of certain animals, usually paired.
hove: verb. To remain suspended in air, water etc.; to float, to hover.
hover: verb. To keep (something, such as an aircraft) in a stationary state in the air.

nah: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Nahuan languages.

oar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Old Aramaic.

ora: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oroha.
orange: noun. An evergreen tree of the genus Citrus such as Citrus sinensis which yields oranges (the fruit).
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
organ: noun. The larger part of an organism, composed of tissues that perform similar functions.
oven: noun. A chamber used for baking or heating.
ran: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Riantana.
range: noun. A line or series of mountains, buildings, etc.
rave: noun. An enthusiastic review (such as of a play).

reg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (Tanzania).

rho: noun. The seventeenth letter of the Modern Greek and Classical alphabets and the nineteenth letter of Old and Ancient.
roan: adjective. Having a coat of a dark base color with individual white hairs mixed in.
